claim: Belgian (Vlaanderen) autumn school holiday in 2026 runs Monday 2 Nov to Sunday 8 Nov, meaning the last week of October (26-30 Oct) is NOT a Belgian school break.
quote: Herfstvakantie 2026 ... van maandag 2 november 2026 tot en met zondag 8 november 2026 ... week 45

claim: European DST ends Sunday 25 October 2026, clocks go back one hour, making evenings noticeably shorter for the rest of the trip window.
quote: All EU countries and most European non-EU countries will set clocks back one hour on Sunday, October 25, 2026

claim: Calima (Saharan dust) events in the Canary Islands trigger official AEMET alerts, can dramatically reduce visibility and air quality, and disrupt flights.
quote: Calima Alerts and Warnings in Canary Islands - Levels and Protocols

claim: Calima episodes regularly disrupt air travel into the Canary Islands; an April 2026 event caused widespread flight delays.
quote: Calima dust storm causes air travel delays in Canary Islands

claim: Madeira and the Azores have been hit by hurricanes between June and November, with the highest risk in September and October.
quote: Madeira has gotten hurricanes in the past between the months of June and November, with the highest risk typically occurring in September and October

claim: Hurricane Leslie (October 2018) prompted a tropical storm warning for Madeira and passed 325 km north-northwest of the island before hitting Portugal — illustrative of the late-October ex-tropical risk.
quote: A tropical storm watch was issued for Madeira on 11 October ... upgraded to a warning at 03:00 UTC on 12 October ... passed 325 km (202 mi) north-northwest of Madeira Island

claim: On 29-30 October 2024 a DANA / gota fría dropped >300 mm of rain in 4 hours over Valencia, killing hundreds — late October is peak gota fría season for eastern Spain.
quote: On the night of 29-30 October 2024, a DANA event caused considerable loss of life and extensive damage, especially in the Valencian Community ... more than 300 millimetres of rainfall during a 4-hour period

claim: Crete's autumn officially starts in October with the first rains; rain becomes more likely especially in the second half of the month.
quote: October is the start of the Cretan autumn and will almost always have some rain, sometimes heavy

claim: Rhodes averages roughly 69 mm of rain over ~5 days in October, with a noticeable cooldown to 20-22 °C by month-end.
quote: Around 69 mm (2.7 in) falls on average, with around 5 days of rain

claim: Many Greek-island resort hotels and beach clubs (e.g. parts of Faliraki on Rhodes, Kos resorts like Lambi/Kardamena) wind down mid-October and have shut by 1 November, while main towns (Rhodes Town, Heraklion, Chania) stay open year-round.
quote: Places catering to tourists will typically close down by November 1st ... Clubs in Faliraki, Rhodes shut down around the 15th October

claim: October is one of Sicily's wettest months with intense showers and sirocco-driven storms; sirocco gusts can reach 100 km/h in autumn.
quote: October is one of the wettest months in Sicily with more than three inches of rain

claim: Sirocco winds reach up to 100 km/h, are most common in autumn and spring, and bring storms and 'blood rain' (red Saharan dust mixed with rain) to southern Italy and Malta.
quote: Sirocco winds with speeds of up to 100 km/h (62 mph) are most common during autumn and spring

claim: Malta's October sees the first proper autumn storms with ~90 mm rain over ~9 days; the gregale wind can hit 90 km/h during these storms.
quote: During storms, the Gregale wind has reached speeds up to 90 km/h

claim: Cyprus in October stays the warmest of the shortlist (25-29 °C, only 2-3 rainy days, ~18 mm in Paphos), the most 'set and forget' weather pick.
quote: October showers in Cyprus are a rarity, with around 2 to 3 rainy days throughout the month

claim: The Algarve stays open all season in October — restaurants, beaches, boat tours all running — with ~80 mm rain over ~10 days, increasing toward month-end.
quote: In October, everything remains open. This is a great time to go hiking, relax on the beach, take a boat or kayaking tour

claim: Madeira's official tourism board maintains a year-round levada/PR walks list, but specific routes can close for rockfalls or storm damage at any time — always check status.
quote: Notice to walkers and list of walks

claim: Madeira in October stays at 20-25 °C with stable trail conditions early in the month, increasing rain risk toward November.
quote: temperatures hover between 20°C and 25°C ... mild and stable weather early in the season and increased chances of rain toward November

claim: Tenerife in October draws growing crowds of northern-European wintering visitors, so prices and bookings rise even though weather stays warm.
quote: October can be a mixed month to visit Tenerife mainly because of the crowds and prices, as it attracts too many people from Europe who come to the island exactly during this month to spend the winter

claim: DANA / gota fría cut-off lows form when warm Mediterranean moisture meets cold upper-air pools — a recurring autumn pattern affecting Spanish-Mediterranean coasts including the Balearics and Costa Blanca.
quote: Cut-off lows, cold drops and DANA
